readingOrder.purpose: Skill entry point — read first for brand essentials and visual identity at a glance
Comprehensive brand reference with content fundamentals, visual foundations, and component patterns
Structured token understanding — programmatic consumption of all design tokens (colors, type, spacing, radius, shadow, layout)
Drop-in runtime CSS variables — link in HTML <head> for all token values; do not read for token understanding when css.json exists
Component index — discover available components (button, input, card, dialog, table, nav) and their slugs
Aggregated component CSS — ready-to-use class definitions for all 6 components extracted from preview pages
Button component reference — visual preview with DOM/CSS, primary/secondary/ghost/danger variants, sm/md/lg sizes
Input component reference — visual preview with DOM/CSS, text/textarea/select/affix variants
Card component reference — visual preview with DOM/CSS, default/interactive/selected states
Dialog component reference — visual preview with DOM/CSS, sm/md sizes, form fields
Table component reference — visual preview with DOM/CSS, sortable columns, selection, status dots
Navigation component reference — visual preview with DOM/CSS, sidebar + tabs variants
Button component contract — intent, variant dimensions, state coverage
Input component contract — intent, variant dimensions, state coverage
Card component contract — intent, variant dimensions, state coverage
Dialog component contract — intent, variant dimensions, state coverage
Table component contract — intent, variant dimensions, state coverage
Navigation component contract — intent, variant dimensions, state coverage
Icon component reference — Lucide (ISC) sprite, sizes 16/20/24, status dual-channel, 51-icon set
Icon component contract — Lucide set, sizes, currentColor, track sources (web=Lucide, macOS=SF Symbols)
Supplementary design tokens specification — user-maintained context
